seoTitle: How to Print From Airtable: Print Table Data
metaDescription: Format Airtable data for printing by hiding fields, adjusting column widths, and using the native print view to export clean PDFs or hard copies.
intro: Airtable's grid view isn't built for paper, but its native print view lets you hide irrelevant columns, adjust sizing, and produce a clean printed or PDF version of your data. This guide covers formatting your table before printing and using the system print dialog to finish the job.

steps.heading: Open your target table
steps.body: Navigate to the table and view containing the data you want to print.

steps.heading: Hide unnecessary fields and rows
steps.body: Hide columns and filter out rows that don't need to appear on the printed page to streamline the layout.

steps.heading: Adjust column and row sizing
steps.body: Resize columns and rows so your data fits cleanly on standard paper dimensions.

steps.heading: Open the print view
steps.body: Use the native print view option from the main view configuration bar to preview how the table will look printed.

steps.heading: Choose orientation and scaling
steps.body: Select portrait or landscape orientation and adjust scaling options to fit your content properly.

steps.heading: Print or export as PDF
steps.body: Launch the system print dialog to send the formatted view to a physical printer or save it as a PDF.

faq.question: Can I print just part of my Airtable data instead of the whole table?
faq.answer: Yes, filtering rows and hiding fields before opening the print view lets you control exactly what shows up on the printed output.

faq.question: Does Airtable have a dedicated print feature?
faq.answer: Yes, there's a native print view accessible from the view configuration bar that's built specifically for formatting grid data for print.

faq.question: Can I save my Airtable table as a PDF instead of printing it?
faq.answer: Yes, the system print dialog usually includes a Save as PDF option depending on your browser and operating system.

faq.question: Why does my printed table look cut off?
faq.answer: This is usually a column width or orientation issue — try switching to landscape and adjusting column widths before printing again.
